what_it_does: This file is the living constitution and drift-home for the file-discovered role registry. It defines what a role is, the schema it follows, how roles self-register from per-file declarations, how slots and model bindings resolve, and enumerates every current role plus conventions and traps.
observations: Roles are self-registering Python files under roles/<id>.py, each exporting a ROLE dict over a C2.1 superset schema.
A role's facet depends on which optional fields are populated: prompt_template+output_schema => generate; op:embed => embed; mode_scope => cast membership; draws => jury.
prompt_slot, schema_slot, and thinking are resolved per turn coordinate rather than being static.
There is an explicit BINDING TRAP warning: default_model must be top-level in ROLE, never nested inside model_binding, or it is silently ignored.

The file lists many canonical roles (judge, listening cast, walkthrough cast, explain_role, refine_decision, verify_jury, guide_author, reduce_synth, repo_digest, register_element, confirm_registration, verify_lens, develop_option, score_options, mine_exchange, judge_mining, decompose_seed, expand_criterion, ground_criterion, triad_synth, judge_drift, dragnet_coarse/fine/design).
Dragnet roles are protected: edit/delete refused, output_schema frozen, schema_slot forbidden, neutral fragment required.
Agent-authored entries (interpret_file, atlas_linker, eval_classify, element_fit_lens, voice_lens) are appended at the bottom with truncated one-line descriptions.
tests/roles_acceptance.py asserts every discovered role is reflected here.
standouts: The binding-trap warning is a strong signal about a real silent failure mode that was caught by-use.
Resolved slots make prompt, schema, and think-control coordinate-dependent rather than static.
Dragnet-family field-freeze door protects a neutral extraction contract from role-level drift.
The file serves as both constitution and drift-home, with acceptance tests forcing synchronization.
concerns: Agent-authored entries have visibly truncated descriptions, suggesting the auto-reflection mechanism may not fully integrate prose.
The binding trap implies other top-level fields could be silently misread if nested; the doc relies on vigilance rather than schema enforcement.
Many roles are described densely; without the acceptance test, this file could drift from the actual ROLE dicts.
No explicit versioning or last-updated timestamp for individual role entries beyond narrative dates.
conventions: Self-registering, file-discovered registry pattern mirroring nodes/.
Constitution-style front matter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status.
Optional schema fields drive role facet rather than explicit role subclasses.
Flat top-level ROLE dict for model bindings; model_binding only holds capability requirements.
No-confidence principle: tags, counts, ordinals, and booleans instead of fake-precision floats.
Never-assert / no-fiction principles for grounding and reuse claims.
Agent-authored entries appended in a dedicated HTML-comment block.
notes: This is the canonical human-readable constitution and inventory for the role registry; it is meant to stay synchronized with discovered roles via acceptance tests.
questions: What exactly enforces the dragnet-family field-freeze door at runtime?
How does the acceptance test handle agent-authored entries whose descriptions are truncated?
Is there a schema-level guard to prevent default_model from being nested inside model_binding?
What determines when an agent-authored role is promoted from the appended block to the main live set?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it documents the role registry, its mechanics, and every role. The appended agent-authored block is a partial reflection, so the 'drift home' claim is slightly uneven in practice.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud runtime validation and no-confidence reporting
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the role layer as declarative, discoverable, and self-extending as the node-type registry, while encoding hard-won operational rules (binding trap, no-fiction, dragnet freeze) as explicit constitution-level warnings.
novelty: The combination of resolved slots (coordinate-driven prompt/schema/think), the binding-trap as a first-class constitutional warning, and the dragnet-family field-freeze door is unusual for a role registry.
contribution: This file contributes a single human-readable constitution and drift-home that records role semantics, binding rules, facet logic, and operational traps, enabling later queries to correlate discovered role files with their intended behavior and constraints.
summary_for_embedding: roles/AGENTS.md is the living constitution and drift-home for the file-discovered role registry, documenting role schema, facet logic, resolved slots, model-binding traps, and every current role including protected dragnet and agent-authored entries.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to keep the role registry understandable, self-extending, and synchronized with runtime discovery through acceptance tests, while encoding binding and no-fiction rules as explicit warnings.
